key_facts: Core O&M subsidiary of Unisun New Energy Group (联盛新能源), formally established 2018
100+ county-level O&M service centers in China
Operations in SE Asia, Middle East, and Europe
Third-party O&M provider — not tied to a single panel or inverter brand
business_model_notes: O&M subsidiary model: Liansheng/Unisun Group develops and acquires solar projects; Uper Energy provides the O&M services layer. Revenue: annual service contracts (per GW managed) + emergency/corrective maintenance calls. 100 county-level centers enable rapid response time for distributed residential and C&I assets — critical for customer retention. The hardware-agnostic approach means Uper can service Sungrow, Growatt, or Chint Anneng installations equally, giving broader TAM than OEM-captive teams.
competitive_position: Dedicated third-party O&M arm with parent-company project pipeline. 1,000 field engineers + 100+ county centers represent substantial fixed-cost investment creating barriers to entry. Competitive threat: major installers (Chint Anneng, Skyworth Solar) increasingly manage their own O&M for their 2M+ installed base, potentially crowding out independent third-party O&M.
research_notes: Medium confidence. Data from Uper Energy website and Tracxn. 2,000 stations and 1,000 engineers from website; independently corroborated by Liansheng corporate disclosure. County-level center count from Uper-energy.com About page. Listed separately from Liansheng-Unisun to track the distinct O&M operational entity.
